evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows OursModel winning on all 5 benchmarks; error bars present; axis labels and benchmark names readable at 7pt.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' are correctly set enabling editable text, but the font rendered is DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) rather than a publication-preferred sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica/Arial/Myriad).
evidence.palette: Uses Wong 2011 colorblind-safe 4-color palette (#999999, #56B4E9, #E69F00, #D55E00) with complementary hatching for B&W readers; no directional green/red misuse.
evidence.layout_density: Y-axis minimum computed dynamically (not forced to 0), 89mm Nature single-column width, tight_layout pad=0.4; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure; legend is 2-column upper-left, frameless; no redundant title; error-bar definition stated; overall presentation is journal-ready.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector output from Matplotlib PDF backend (pdf.fonttype=42, CIDFontType2 embedded TrueType); text is selectable and not rasterized.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (64 KB), figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names all 5 benchmarks, identifies winning method, and quantifies the gain as 3.3–13.1 percentage points over the strongest baseline; dual-encoding rationale explained.

overall_strengths: Excellent palette and dual encoding (color + hatch) ensures accessibility for colorblind and grayscale readers.
Caption is specific and quantitative, directly supporting the scientific claim with a numerical range.
overall_weaknesses: Font family defaults to DejaVu Sans; a journal submission would benefit from switching to Helvetica, Arial, or another publication-standard sans-serif.
No explicit data source or seed count mentioned in the caption (e.g., 'five random seeds' implies reproducibility but n is not stated in the axis or legend).
